questions.question: Describe the difference between a socket's bind address and its listen address. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: The bind address is the address that the socket is bound to, whereas the listen address is the address that incoming connections are listened on.

questions.question: A TCP/IP socket in the listening state is waiting for incoming connections from a client. What is the purpose of the socket's backlog queue?
questions.hint: The backlog queue holds pending connections that are waiting to be accepted by the server.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the socket's timeout option in the setsockopt function? Provide an example code snippet to demonstrate how to set a timeout.
questions.hint: The timeout option sets a limit on the amount of time that a socket will wait for data to arrive from a remote host.
